judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are thorough and hit all four expectations with solid evidence; Report 2 is slightly more concise while Report 1 adds a few extra flagged items (production-data privacy concern, unsubstantiated stat) — overall comparable quality.

report_1.evidence: **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default." — FALSE.** `app/security/config.py` reads: `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` ... **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF." — FALSE / not implemented.** `app/routers/visits.py` contains only a `list_visits` route that returns `[]`, followed by `# TODO: pdf export`. The PDF export does not exist. **Must be removed.**

report_2.evidence: **"Export your visit history … Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** — False. `app/routers/visits.py` implements only `GET /visits`, which returns `[]`, followed by `# TODO: pdf export` (line 11)... **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** — False; directly contradicted by code. `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic."

report_1.evidence: `captures.json` line 3: `"after_scheduling.png": {"route": "/billing/invoices", ...}` — the "after" was captured from the **billing/invoices** route, not the calendar. Opening the images confirms it visually: `before_scheduling.png` reads **"SCHEDULING CALENDAR"**, but `after_scheduling.png` reads **"BILLING / INVOICES"** — a completely unrelated screen.

report_2.evidence: `captures.json` records `"after_scheduling.png": {"route": "/billing/invoices", "state": "seed-demo"}`, whereas `before_scheduling.png` is `{"route": "/schedule", ...}`. The image content confirms it: `before_scheduling.png` renders the label "SCHEDULING CALENDAR" but `after_scheduling.png` renders "BILLING / INVOICES".

report_1.evidence: **Broken reference.** Not on disk; never committed (`git show --stat HEAD` lists no such file). `captures.json` even labels it `"expected capture; no corresponding file"`... unused_capture_07.png | **NO** | yes | **Orphan.** Exists on disk but is referenced nowhere in the document; opening it shows a "PATIENT LIST" screen unrelated to any section.

report_2.evidence: **`after_export.png` — referenced but missing.** The doc references `![after](img/after_export.png)` (line 26), but no such file exists in `docs/release/img/` ... **`unused_capture_07.png` — exists but unused.** The file is present in `img/` but is referenced nowhere in the document; its content reads "PATIENT LIST."

report_1.evidence: **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** This is internal process metadata. ... Draft/AI-assembly notes should not appear in a finished customer email.

report_2.evidence: **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance." (line 33)** — an internal drafting note; it should be removed from a customer-facing email.
